content.full_text:
Lieutenant Governor
Leo McCarthy
State of California

November 28, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Margaret Ellison

To Whom It May Concern:

I would like to request the opinion of the Fair Political Practices Commission on a matter involving potential royalties from publication of a document my office helped prepare.

Along with U.S. Senator Alan Cranston and U.S. Representative Mel Levine, I recently convened and co-chaired an extragovernmental organization known as the California-Pacific Year 2000 Task Force. This organization was associated with my office through the Commission for Economic Development (CED) which I chair.

One of the co-chairs has expressed an interest in offering the final report of the Task Force for private publication in Japan. The publishers in question would in all likelihood offer an advance and royalty payments.

My questions are as follows:

1) Would funds generated by this proposed publication be considered funds due to the State of California?

   a) If so, should they be applied to the Commission for Economic Development’s funds?

   b) If not, what would the legal requirements be for disposition of the funds?

2) Would private publication of this document conflict with any other FPPC regulations?

Fair Political Practices Commission  
November 28, 1990  
Page 2

Thank you for your assistance in resolving these issues. Please contact Ann Mills or Jason Warburg at (916) 445-8994 if you have any questions regarding this request.

Sincerely,

Leo McCarthy

LM:jw
